#AreaNews | La Union Sets the Standard with Successful M&E Coaching Sessions and Pilot Testing

NYC Baguio Area Office successfully conducted the Local Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Coaching Sessions and pilot testing of the Functionality Assessment Tool. The event, held on September 4, 2024, in collaboration with the City Government of San Fernando, La Union, showcased a significant step forward in enhancing the effectiveness of youth development programs across the region.

The one-day event was attended by 21 Local Youth Development Officers (LYDOs), 21 Sangguniang Kabataan Provincial Federation (SKPP) members, and 21 Local Youth Development Council (LYDC) representatives from various local government units (LGUs). The sessions, supported by the City Government of San Fernando La Union, featured key discussions led by Dr. Sheridan Athena Y. Gajete and Mr. Dave Homer Ariola from the NYC Baguio Area Office.

Dr. Gajete provided an in-depth overview of the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028, including planning guidelines, roles and functions of LYDCs and LYDOs, and updates on Sangguniang Kabataan (SK). She also introduced the SK Functionality Assessment Tool, designed to evaluate and improve the operational efficiency of SKs. Mr. Ariola addressed monitoring guidelines, updates on local government units, and the web portal.

The M&E framework established during the sessions aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and inclusivity in decision-making processes. By collecting and analyzing grassroots-level data, the framework provides policymakers with a comprehensive view of the socioeconomic factors affecting youth. This data-driven approach ensures that national and local youth policies are both responsive and effective.

During the afternoon session, participants engaged in a workshop where they reviewed and discussed the draft SK Functionality Assessment Tool. Feedback included suggestions to streamline the tool, consider variations in LGU sizes, and incorporate sanctions for non-compliance. The discussions emphasized the need for a more efficient and relevant assessment process.

The day concluded with a successful benchmarking session with the City Youth Development Office of San Fernando City, La Union, which highlighted the progress of teen centers across 46 of 48 barangays. Ms. Zyjazz de Guzman delivered the closing remarks, emphasizing the importance of a strong partnership between SKs and LYDOs for effective compliance and performance.

The M&E coaching sessions and pilot testing mark a significant advancement in local youth development efforts, setting a precedent for future initiatives in the region.

#AreaNews | Sulu Youth Summit 2024: Empowering the Next Generation Through Digital Pathways

The 3rd Sulu Youth Summit 2024, organized by the Sulu Provincial Government in collaboration with the Provincial Youth Development Office and the Sangguniang Kabataan Provincial Federation, commenced on August 28 at the Sumadja Hall, Sulu Area Coordinating Center in Patikul. This two-day event, held in celebration of Youth Empowerment Month and the "Linggo ng Kabataan," adopted the theme, "From Clicks to Progress: Youth Digital Pathways for Sustainable Development."

The first day featured discussions on the transformative power of digital tools, focusing on leadership and youth empowerment. Notable presentations included "Social Media and Its Impact" by DICT Project Development Officer Ryan S. Hammari and "Social Media: Treat or Threat" by RAPA-YSO Vice-President Almudzrine Saribbon, highlighting both the potential and risks of social media.

In an interactive talk show hosted by Noel Alih and Arweda Misal from the Batah Sug Advocate for Youth Empowerment, participants were encouraged to voice their opinions on pressing youth issues, fostering a sense of solidarity and collective action.

Distinguished guests such as SKF President Yusofkhan J. Imbing, SK Focal Person Adnan Mohammad, Former TAYO President Allen Talikan, and Maimbung LYDO Nickmar Asjali played active roles in guiding the discussions, inspiring youth leaders to engage in meaningful community work.

On the second day, the summit delved into strategic discussions about the youth's role in nation-building. Mr. Mayjee A. De La Cruz gave a comprehensive presentation on the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028, stressing the importance of aligning youth efforts with national development goals.

A hands-on field visit to Barangay Poblacion, Maimbung, offered participants a firsthand look at grassroots involvement. This visit allowed them to engage with local Sangguniang Kabataan officials and witness the positive impact of effective youth leadership at the barangay level.

As the summit concluded, participants left with a renewed sense of purpose, equipped to lead their communities toward sustainable development in the digital age.

#AreaNews | CAR Sets New Standards with Migration and Development Information Sharing System

Exciting developments are underway for the Cordillera Administrative Region (CAR) as the Regional Population Executive Board (RPEB-CAR) advances its flagship initiative, the Migration and Development Information Sharing System (ISS). During its third-quarter meeting on September 4, 2024, the RPEB-CAR adopted key indicators to drive the launch of this transformative program.

The ISS is set to improve how migration data is managed and utilized in CAR. The newly approved indicators will encompass internal and international migration trends, the socio-demographic profiles of repatriates, foreign nationals, and emigrant statistics. This data will be crucial for updating the Cordillera Regional Development Plan (RDP) 2023-2028 and will enhance the decision-making process for local plans and policies.

The ISS will serve as a comprehensive portal for government agencies, Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs), prospective OFWs, researchers, and other stakeholders, which is scheduled to be launched on November 2024. It will also provide up-to-date information and resources on migration issues, reinforcing the region's commitment to data-driven policy-making.

In addition to the ISS, the RPEB-CAR has outlined several major activities for 2024, including advocacy for the Regional POPDEV Plan of Action and the Regional POPDEV Summit, where the ISS will be officially unveiled. The board will continue to work towards integrating population and development (POPDEV) considerations into major government programs throughout CAR.

The RPEB-CAR, under the leadership of NEDA-CAR, is dedicated to advancing population management and migration policies in the region, ensuring that CAR remains at the forefront of effective and innovative solutions for migration and development.

PRESS RELEASE | NATIONAL ADVISORY COUNCIL CONVENES FOR 3RD MEETING; EDUCATION SECRETARY SONNY ANGARA SIGNS IRR OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11913 OR â€œNATIONAL YOUTH DAYâ€ ACT

The National Advisory Council (NAC) held its 3rd meeting on October 4, 2024, marking a significant milestone as the first in-person gathering since the pandemic and with the newly appointed officials of the Commission. The meeting was presided over by NAC Chair and DepEd Philippines Secretary Sonny Angara, with the NAC  secretariat led by NYC Chairperson and CEO USec. Jeff Ortega.

Key government agencies were represented, including the Department of Labor and Employment - DOLE, 
Department of Agriculture - Philippines (DA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of Social Welfare and Development - DSWD, Department of Foreign Affairs, Republic of the Philippines (DFA), Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) DILG Philippines, National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA), Philippine Sports Commission (PSC), Technical Education and Skills Development Authority (TESDA), and Commission on Higher Education(CHED)

During the meeting, the NYC presented updates on the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028 and its Monitoring and Evaluation Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Framework, Magna Carta for Out-of-School Youth, and the latest developments on Sangguniang Kabataan-related activities.

A major highlight of the meeting was the signing of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 11913, otherwise known as the "National Youth Day Act," by Education Secretary Sonny Angara. The signing of the IRR paves the way for the effective implementation of the law, which aims to recognize the vital role of the youth in nation-building and to ensure their meaningful participation in society.

The IRR outlines the guidelines and mechanisms for the observance of National Youth Day on August 12 of every year, including the conduct of various activities and programs to empower and engage the youth. It also defines key terms, establishes the responsibilities of government agencies, and provides for the necessary budget and resources to support the implementation of the law.

"This successful conduct of the NAC meeting served as a platform for us to align our efforts and ensure that the needs and aspirations of the Filipino youth are addressed effectively," NYC Chairperson Ortega stated as he emphasized the importance of collaborating with key stakeholders through dialogues and meetings like the NAC meeting.

The 3rd NAC meeting was also joined by NYC Commissioners-at-Large ASec. Karl Josef Legazpi and ASec. Michelle Mae Gonzales, Commissioner representing Luzon Atty. Reena Vivienne Pineda, SK National Representative and Ex-Officio Commissioner Hon. Carol Julianne Dalipe CJ Dalipe-Page, Executive Director Leah Villalon, and SK Island Representatives of Luzon and Visayas Hon. Angelica Alcala and Hon. Edmundo Tolentino Jr..

#AreaNews | Promoting Inclusivity: NYC's Insightful Gender Fair Language Seminar for AFP 1st CRG CAR

The 1st Civil Relations Group (CRG) of the Armed Forces of the Philippines recently held a comprehensive Gender and Development (GAD) and Gender Fair Language Training session for both uniformed and non-uniformed personnel. The training aimed to enhance understanding and implementation of gender fair language (GFL), with Dr. Sheridan Athena Gajete from the National Youth Commission Baguio Area Office serving as the resource speaker.

Dr. Gajete's presentation focused on the principles and strategies of gender fair language, emphasizing its role in reducing gender stereotyping and discrimination. She explained that GFL is achieved through two main strategies: neutralization and feminization. Neutralization involves replacing gender-specific terms, such as "policeman," with gender-neutral alternatives like "police officer." Feminization, on the other hand, involves using feminine forms to ensure visibility for female referents, such as "he or she" instead of simply "he."

During her session, Dr. Gajete highlighted the importance of integrating research on language structures, policies, and individual behavior to understand how GFL can mitigate gender biases. She stressed that promoting inclusive language requires a willingness to admit mistakes, listen, ask questions, and understand the historical context of certain terms. Impolite, disrespectful, or discriminatory language can alienate colleagues and negatively impact workplace relationships, whereas inclusive language fosters a more diverse and welcoming environment.

Dr. Gajete concluded her presentation by underscoring that gender equality is not merely a womenâ€™s issue but a matter that affects everyone. Her insights provided valuable guidance for the AFP 1st CRG CAR personnel in fostering a more inclusive and equitable environment.

#AreaNews | Radyo Pilipinas Hosts 7th Kabataan Kaagapay sa Kaunlaran Interview

On August 8, 2024, Radyo Pilipinas conducted its 7th interview for the "Kabataan Kaagapay sa Kaunlaran" (KKK) program, hosted by station manager Sir Andre Amadeo. This monthly radio show, airing every second Thursday, highlights the initiatives and efforts of Filipino youth in their development journey.

The August interview featured updates on National Youth Commission (NYC) programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) presented by Ms. Sheridan Athena Gajete. She detailed the latest guidelines from TESDA, CHED, LYDC elections, filling up of vacancies, and special elections. Additionally, she provided insights into significant activities from July to August.

A notable update was the NYC Resolution endorsing the Magna Carta for Young Farmers. This proposed legislation aims to recognize the vital role of young individuals in agriculture and their contribution to the country's food security. Another highlight was the upcoming Linggo ng Kabataan 2024, set to be celebrated during the week of August 12th, encompassing International Youth Day, ASEAN Youth Day, and National Youth Day. The theme for International Youth Day 2024, "From Clicks to Progress: Youth Digital Pathways for Sustainable Development," emphasizes the importance of digitalization in achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the significant contributions of youth in this area.

Ms. Gajete also discussed a series of training sessions for Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) and Local Youth Development Officers (LYDOs). She shared intriguing youth trivia, noting that half of the global population is under 30, with this percentage expected to rise to 57% by 2030. She highlighted a survey indicating that 67% of people believe in a better future, with the most optimism coming from 15-17-year-olds. Additionally, she pointed out the imbalance in political representation, with only 2.6% of parliamentarians globally being under 30, and less than 1% of these young parliamentarians being women.

NYC Baguio Area invited the Lupon ng mga Indibidwal na Nangangalaga sa Kalikasan (LINK), represented by Mark Ryan Talajuron, to discuss their mission as youth advocates aiming to build an empowered and environmentally friendly community.

After serving the in the NYC for six (6) years, outgoing NYC Chairman Ronald Cardema received the coveted Honorary Navy Seal Badge from the elite Naval Special Operations Command (NAVSOCOM), Sangley Point, Cavite. As a youth leader, NYC Chairman Ronald Cardema served as:

The National Youth Commission (NYC) through the Cebu Area Office, attended and provided technical assistance in the conduct of the SK Planning, Budgeting, and Leadership Seminar-Workshop 2024 for the SK officials of Gandara, Samar, held in Calbayog City. The activity was attended by 100 participants, including SK Chairpersons, Secretaries, Treasurers, and members of various barangays from the said municipality.

Opening remarks were delivered by the SK Federation President of the Municipality of Gandara, Samar, Hon. Christine C. Avila, through an AVP. In her speech, she extended her heartfelt gratitude to the SK officials from different barangays who took the time to attend the activity. She also mentioned that this activity is crucial for SK officials, empowering them in leadership, CBYDP planning, and preparing and increasing awareness in monitoring and evaluation.

During the first day of the 2-day activity, the following topics and their resource speakers were discussed: Topic 1: "Being a Leader, a Role in Nation-Building" was presented by Ms. Jonah Salcedo, Planning Coordinator II of NWSSU in Calbayog. Ms. Cherry Mae Malate, Presidential Staff Officer IV of the Cebu Area Office, presented Topic 3: "PYDP (Philippine Youth Development Plan) 2023-2028" to the SK officials of Gandara, Samar. She highlighted the importance of the national plan, which is also incorporated into the 10 Centers of Youth Participation. Ms. Pamela Aliman, LYDO of Gandara, Samar, presented the Monitoring and Evaluation process, timeline, legal bases, M&E forms, and how it is conducted from the grassroots level up to the national level, emphasizing its importance for SK officials. She also led the workshop on crafting the CBYDP and ABYIP for each SK barangay official on the second day. The 2-day activity concluded with the awarding of certificates to the speakers and participants, followed by a closing message from the LYDO of Gandara, Samar.

In a crucial development for juvenile justice in the region, the RJJWC Region III recently convened at the ACTION Inc. Office in Olongapo City to finalize the draft Comprehensive Regional Juvenile Intervention Program (CRJIP) 2024-2028. This multi-sectoral and inter-agency initiative aims to address the needs of children at risk and those in conflict with the law by focusing on their rehabilitation and reintegration into their families and communities.

Ms. Imelda E. Yumul welcomed the attendees, outlined the meeting's objectives, and conducted a Situational Analysis (SitAn) presentation to set the stage for the discussions.

Following the SitAn, committee members contributed their insights and comments, leading to an in-depth discussion on the CRJIP. Ms. Yumul presented the draft program, which was followed by a breakout session where members worked on refining the CRJIP result matrix.

The session culminated in the presentation and agreement on the final CRJIP Result Matrix. Ms. Yumul concluded the meeting with closing remarks, and participants captured the moment with a group photo.

The finalized CRJIP 2024-2028 represents a significant step forward in enhancing regional efforts to support and rehabilitate at-risk youth, ensuring a coordinated approach to juvenile justice and welfare.

#AreaNews | Strengthening Regional Health Initiatives: TB-HIV RCC Meeting Focuses on Collaborative Prevention Efforts

The TB-HIV Regional Coordinating Committee (RCC) recently convened to advance the Regional AIDS, STIs, Viral Hepatitis, and Tuberculosis Prevention and Control Program, with a shared vision of a TB and HIV-free Philippines. Hosted at the Bendix Hotel in the City of San Fernando, Pampanga, the meeting brought together key stakeholders to discuss and enhance regional health strategies.

The session began with formalities, including an invocation and the singing of the National Anthem. Participants were introduced, representing a broad spectrum of health and community organizations.

Ms. Geliza D. Recede, RN, the Regional TB-HIV Nurse Coordinator, opened the meeting with a call for active engagement and productive discussions. She expressed optimism about the groupâ€™s ability to make meaningful progress and reach consensus.

JO2 Anna Lalaine Casaje, Nurse Staff of the BJMP's Health and Sanitation Division, reported on the achievements in active case-finding, detailing TB screenings conducted in provincial jails, totaling 5,297 individuals.

Ms. Danika Joy Bardelosa from OneImpact Philippines introduced the organizationâ€™s innovative public health solutions and encouraged members to utilize and promote their app, available on both Play Store and Apple Store. OneImpact aims to enhance public health through partnerships across Central Luzon.

Mr. Giorgo Arnel Alonzo, RN, Program Health Coordinator from Clark Development Corporation, provided updates on their TB-HIV awareness campaigns, noting successful diagnostics of 92 TB cases through annual physical exams and enhanced healthcare access via PhilHealth Konsulta.

Ms. Recede presented the latest social behavior change communication plan for TB and HIV, stressing the ongoing challenges of high TB burden in the Philippines. She highlighted the importance of widespread awareness, prevention, and access to quality treatment to achieve the goal of a TB-free nation.

Representatives from various agencies, including DOST, DILG, TB Heals and Support Group, TB People of the Philippines, PhilHealth, Central Luzon OSH Network, NYC, and DSWD, shared updates and participated in discussions. Ms. Rose Chell Anne C. Rojas, Head of the Pampanga Area Office of the NYC, provided insights into RA 10742, focusing on aligning local and regional health plans with broader youth development strategies.

The meeting concluded with a photo opportunity capturing the collective commitment of all participants. Agreements made during the session will be shared and disseminated by the secretariat to ensure continued collaboration and progress in regional health initiatives.

#AreaNews | Youth Leaders Engage in SK Consultation and YORP-LMS Training in Sorsogon City

The Sorsogon Provincial Gymnasium in Sorsogon City recently served as the venue for a pivotal capability-building event, bringing together Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) officials for a comprehensive consultation and orientation session.

The SK Consultation aimed to provide a platform for local SK officials to address and resolve operational concerns. Representatives from key agencies, including the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), National Youth Commission (NYC), Technical Education and Skills Development Authority (TESDA), Commission on Audit (COA), Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP), and Land Bank of the Philippines, participated as resource persons. The session attracted approximately 3,000 SK officials from across the province. Key issues discussed included filling vacant positions, addressing concerns related to the National Service Training Program (NSTP), the turnover of responsibilities from previous SK officials, budgetary concerns, disallowance issues, and the need for bookkeeping orientation for newly appointed SK Treasurers. Additionally, there were discussions on school attendance for SK officials and inactive SK councils.

The event was graced by Governor Boboy Hamor, who highlighted the importance of addressing these concerns to enhance the effectiveness of SK officials in their roles. Resource persons not only addressed the issues raised but also provided updates and promoted various programs and activities relevant to the SK officials.

The second part of the event focused on the orientation for the Youth Organization Registration Program (YORP) and Learning Management System (LMS). Ms. Nydia Delfin led the orientation, presenting data on provincial LGU accounts with YORP Hub and LMS accounts. She provided a thorough overview of the legal basis, purpose, objectives, registration process, and requirements for YORP and LMS. The role of Youth Development Officers (YDOs), Local Youth Development Councils (LYDCs), and SK Portals in these systems was emphasized.

Participants raised several concerns, including the possibility of submitting SEC registrations instead of constitutions and by-laws for confidentiality reasons, difficulties with uploading large files to YORP, lack of turnover from previous YDOs, and issues with recalling YORP Hub account passwords. New YDOs also inquired about the process for accreditation as Training Managers for SKMT. Ms. Delfin addressed these concerns and shared updates on SK policies.

The consultation and orientation were organized by the Local Government of Sorsogon through the Provincial Youth Development Office, led by Mr. Julius Edma, in coordination with SK Federation President KM Lozada. The successful execution of these events underscores the provinceâ€™s commitment to equipping its youth leaders with the knowledge and skills necessary for effective community engagement and development.

NYC Chairman Ronald Cardema and Congresswoman Drixie Mae Cardema successfully defended the National Youth Commission and its strong stand against remaining NPA youth recruitment in schools throughout our nation.

Sa kauna-unahang pagkakataon sa 20 years, hindi na nagtanong ang tatlong makakaliwang kongresista sa kanilang harapan mismo dahil sa strong stand ni NYC Chairman Cardema laban sa NPA at sa mga makakaliwang kongresista sa harapan nila na ayaw umamin at ikondena ang lampas kalhating libong myembro nila at kaalyado nila na napatay na bilang NPA Fighters sa Pilipinas.

Sa anim na taon ni NYC Chairman Ronald Cardema, naibalik ang Sangguniang Kabataan (SK), pinartner ang mga SK Youth Leaders sa mga Kadete ng PMA, PNPA, PPSA, PMMA, MAAP, OCS, at ROTC, nagpartner sa laban ng AFP & PNP sa NPA na mula sa 89 Guerilla Units ng NPA noong 2016 ngayon ay 7 (pito) na lang. Successful na paglaban sa impluwensya at recruitment ng NPA sa ating mga anak, apo, at kabataang Pilipino.

Nais rin ni NYC Chairman Ronald Cardema maibalik ang Mandatory Boy Scouts & Girl Scouts for elementary students, Mandatory CAT for High School Students, at Mandatory ROTC for college students para ma-orient ang kabataang Pilipino patungkol sa disiplina, pagmamahal sa bayan, at disaster preparedness dahil maraming bagyo at baha sa ating bansa at sila ay tunay na magiging pag-asa ng kanilang pamilya, kanilang komunidad, at pag-asa ng bayan sa panahon ng emergencies at sakuna. Mabuhay, National Youth Commission at mga Kabataang Pilipino!

#AreaNews | Training for Local Leaders and Educators on YDS Modules Empowers Youth Development

A recent training session focused on enhancing local youth development programs brought together City/Municipal Links, Coordinators, DepED Designated Guidance Counselors, and Youth Development Formation Focal points. The event aimed to familiarize participants with the Youth Development Session (YDS) Modules and the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028.

Ms. Nydia P. Delfin, NYC Naga Area Officer, served as the key resource person for the session. She delivered a comprehensive overview of the PYDP 2023-2028, outlining its significance as the national framework for youth development. Her presentation highlighted the PYDPâ€™s 10 Centers of Participation, addressing key youth issues, strategies, and priority programs, projects, and activities (PPAs). Ms. Delfin also discussed the localization of the PYDP and its integration with local youth programs, emphasizing the importance of engaging with the Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) and Local Youth Development Office (LYDO). Additionally, she introduced the City-Based Youth Development Plan (CBYDP) and the Local Youth Development Plan (LYDP) as essential local counterparts to the PYDP, along with updates on the PYDP.

In her closing remarks, Ms. Delfin expressed her appreciation to the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) 4Ps Division for facilitating the dissemination of the PYDP. She praised the participants for their critical role in youth development, acknowledging their frontline efforts in directly supporting the youth within their communities.

The primary goal of the training was to prepare partner implementers for school-based YDS, enabling them to become subject matter experts who will assist City/Municipal Links in conducting effective sessions. By expanding the pool of YDS facilitators, the program seeks to provide a more customized approach to meet the diverse developmental needs of youth across various age groups. This tailored approach aims to enhance the effectiveness of youth interventions, catering specifically to the needs of teenagers, preteens, and young adults.

#AreaNews | RJJWC and RSCWC Convened to Shape Future Initiatives

The 2nd Joint Meeting of the Regional Juvenile Justice and Welfare Committee (RJJWC) and the Regional Sub-Committee for the Welfare of Children (RSCWC) recently convened at Madison Hotel under the leadership of Mr. Norman S. Laurio, DSWD Regional Director and RJJWC Chairperson.

Region 5 marked a milestone with its first TESDA NC II graduates from among its center clientele. Additionally, members were reminded to finalize their signatures on previous resolutions and were encouraged to participate in the upcoming National Childrenâ€™s Month and JJWeek celebrations.

A significant resolution was passed to include the Regional Alternative Child Care Office V (RACCO) as a special member of the RSCWC. The committee also addressed feedback on the Local Council for the Protection of Children Functionality Assessment results and provided input on the Child-Friendly Local Governance Audit indicators.

Support for the Magna Carta for Children and the Philippines Children Commission Bill was affirmed with a passed resolution. Members were instructed to note the schedules for the second semester's activities.

In other updates, the NYC will appoint a new youth representative to replace Mr. Jhonel De Lima, who is stepping down due to other commitments. The NYC will also coordinate with the central office for Childrenâ€™s Month activities, where it will play a leading role.

The meeting concluded with a shared commitment to advancing the welfare of children and youth in the region.

READ | National Youth Commission Supports House Bill No. 6066 for Establishment of Resource Development and Crisis Assistance Centers

The National Youth Commission (NYC) expressed its support for House Bill No. 6066, which seeks to establish Resource Development and Crisis Assistance Centers in every province and city across the country. 

The bill aims to empower women and children through skills development, capacity building, and livelihood programs and the NYC believes that this initiative aligns with the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028, which prioritizes the protection of youth from discrimination, abuse, and exploitation.

The proposed centers aim to provide essential services for women and children, including skills training, livelihood support, and crisis assistance. These services are crucial in addressing issues such as poverty, violence, and exploitation, which disproportionately affect young people.

While the NYC commends the bill's objectives, it also emphasizes the importance of efficient resource utilization. The Commission suggests exploring opportunities to integrate the proposed centers with existing government facilities, such as those established under Republic Act No. 8505 (Rape Victim Assistance and Protection Act of 1998), to optimize resource allocation and maximize impact.

NYC is committed to working closely with lawmakers and other stakeholders to ensure that the needs of youth are fully considered in the implementation of the said HB.

Despite the challenges posed by Typhoon Carina, the Baguio Area Office of the National Youth Commission (NYC) successfully conducted a two-day Youth Development through Leadership Training in Paracelis, one of the most remote areas in the Cordillera Administrative Region (CAR).

The training brought together 34 participants from various sectors including Local Youth Development Officers (LYDOs), Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) Federation members, partner agencies, and Local Youth Development Council (LYDC) representatives from the nine barangays of the municipality.

Hosted by LYDO Jo Anne Cabanes Gullunan, the event featured a comprehensive program covering a range of topics essential for youth leadership and development. Discussions included Servant Leadership, Youth Leadership, the Youth Offender Rehabilitation Program (YORP), SK Updates, NYC Programs and Projects, and the roles and functions of LYDOs, SKs, and LYDCs. Additionally, participants learned about the guidelines for monitoring and evaluating the implementation of the Local Youth Development Plan (LYDP), Comprehensive Barangay Youth Development Plan (CBYDP), and Annual Barangay Youth Investment Program (ABYIP).

The welcome message was delivered by Emma Tamang, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), who expressed gratitude for the participants' dedication despite the adverse weather conditions. She commended the NYC for their commitment to reaching Paracelis. Atty. Larry Segnaben, Executive Assistant IV from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Dr. Marcos Ayangwa, delivered an inspirational message emphasizing the significance of leadership training in shaping responsible future leaders and the importance of prioritizing education.

LYDC advisers from DepEd Paracelis, Maâ€™am Zenaida Mablay and Bailyne Faryangan, also attended the training, contributing to the depth of discussions.

The sessions were led by Ms. Gajete, who covered topics including leadership concepts, the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) 2023-2028, DILG Memorandum Circular 151-2019, commitment setting, and advocacy building. Dave Homer Ariola, Presidential Officer Staff I of NYC-Baguio Area, presented on Servant Leadership, YORP, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020, and provided updates on SK initiatives.

The training included interactive workshops, icebreakers, and commitment-building activities, effectively engaging the participants and fostering a proactive approach to youth leadership.

The NYC Baguio teamâ€™s successful mission in Paracelis underscored the importance of youth empowerment and leadership development in even the most challenging circumstances.

#AreaNews | PYDP Regional Taskforce Holds 2nd Semester Meeting in Catanduanes

The Provincial Capitol Conference Room in Virac, Catanduanes, recently hosted the second-semester meeting of the PYDP Regional Taskforce. The meeting aimed to discuss progress, address issues, and strategize for the effective implementation of the Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) and related initiatives.

The session began with institutional updates on the PYDP 2023-2028 and SK policies, including a presentation of the Functionality Assessment Toolkit for SK and LYDO. The task force recommended including LYDC resolutions and certifications from local government units as part of the Minimum Operating Values (MOVs) for approved LYDPs. Members were encouraged to review and provide feedback on the toolkit by July 15, 2024.

Regional data on Local Youth Development Officers (LYDO), Local Youth Development Plans (LYDP), and LYDCs, as well as SK Web Portal/LMS-YDO and SK registration status, were reviewed. The secretariat requested copies of the Local Youth Development Plans from provinces that had yet to submit theirs and sought assistance in gathering LYDP and LYDC minutes from the respective LGUs. Members were advised to compile provincial data to track and assist their LGUs effectively, including YDO and SK Federation registration.

The meeting also covered updates from the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG). The secretariat requested a copy of the DILG presentation to ensure alignment with NYC data.

Members reviewed regional youth data from the YouthLed Survey, with encouragement to use this data for planning and reviewing their initiatives. PYDOs committed to supporting Hirayang Kabataan activities and requested to submit proposals and coordinate during budget preparations to secure resource allocation.

The task force also pledged support for the National Youth Agriculture Summit organized by Albay Young Farmers and requested a meeting with the organizers to facilitate assistance.

In discussing regional HIV/AIDS status, GentleMen Bicol offered its expertise and resources to support PYDOs and SKs in implementing HIV/AIDS and Adolescent Reproductive Health (ARH) programs. This support includes providing speakers, technical assistance, educational materials, and potential venue options. Members were urged to lobby for the reactivation of the Local AIDS Council and include LYDOs and SK Federations as council members. GentleMen Bicol committed to providing technical assistance for this initiative and encouraged prioritization of HIV/AIDS activities.

Lastly, the meeting addressed the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps) youth data. The inclusion of YDOs and SK Federations in the program's advisory council at various levels was discussed, aiming to enhance support and adaptation of YDS modules. Members were urged to engage with 4Ps beneficiaries in their programs and projects. GentleMen Bicol also committed to collaborating with the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) on gender mainstreaming and HIV/AIDS education for Indigenous Peoples groups.

The meeting underscored a collaborative approach to advancing youth development and governance, ensuring that stakeholders are aligned and equipped to address the challenges and opportunities within their communities.

The certification, given by the Civil Service Commission (CSC), recognizes the NYC's adherence to rigorous standards in its HR policies and processes, achieved through the collective efforts of all divisions and offices of the NYC, including various committees such as the Performance Management Team and the Human Resource Merit Promotion and Selection Board.

The awarding ceremony, held at the National Youth Commission TAYO Conference Room, began with opening remarks delivered by NYC Commissioner representing Luzon, Atty. Reena Vivienne Pineda. Director of the Civil Service Commission - Office of the President Cluster, Dir. Roderick J. Romulo, extended an inspiring message following the awarding of the Prime-HRM Level 2 Certification.

Prime-HRM (Program to Institutionalize Meritocracy and Excellence in Human Resource Management) Level 2 is a mechanism that empowers government agencies by developing HR systems aimed at improving efficiency, transparency, and service delivery. The NYC's attainment of this certification highlights its dedication to fostering a conducive work environment and promoting merit-based principles across its operations.

The NYC continues to strive towards further elevating its organizational capabilities to better meet the needs of the youth sector it serves.

READ | National Youth Commission Backs Proposed Amendment to Enhance the RA 8505: Rape Victim Assistance and Protection Act of 1998

In a Commission resolution approved to bolster support for victims of sexual violence, the National Youth Commission (NYC) has unanimously endorsed proposed amendments to Republic Act No. 8505, known as the Rape Victim Assistance and Protection Act of 1998.

The NYC's support extends to House Bill Numbers (HBN) 5186, 4967, 6393, 8237, and 8676, which collectively aim to strengthen provisions crucial to aiding victims. Key amendments include the provision of Rape and Sexual Assault Kits in every police precinct, facilitating swifter evidence collection. Guidelines and Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) will be developed by the PNP in collaboration with the DOH, ensuring efficient kit utilization.

Further amendments propose the establishment of Resource Development and Crisis Assistance Centers across provinces and cities, spearheaded by a collaborative effort involving DSWD, DOH, DILG, DOJ, and accredited NGOs. These centers will be complemented by an Advisory Council comprising representatives from various governmental bodies and NGOs, ensuring comprehensive support and guidance.

The NYC underscores that the introduction of specialized Rape and Assault Kits will significantly enhance the implementation of RA 8505, addressing critical gaps in current response mechanisms. Emphasizing the importance of tailored solutions, the Commission advocates for kits specifically designed to meet the unique needs of rape and assault victims.

This initiative reflects the NYC's commitment to safeguarding the welfare of Filipino youth, championing legislative reforms that promise more effective support and protection for victims of sexual violence.

READ | NYC CHAMPIONS CLIMATE ACTION THROUGH NATIONAL ACE STRATEGY 

The National Youth Commission (NYC) recently passed a resolution expressing its strong support for the development and mainstreaming of the National Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Strategy Plan in the Philippines. The resolution aims to empower young Filipinos to be active participants in climate change solutions.

"As a nation particularly vulnerable to the effects of climate change, it is crucial to equip our youth with the knowledge and skills to take action in ensuring a Whole-of-Nation approach,â€ said Commissioner representing Visayas and Philippine Youth Development Plan (PYDP) Committee on Environment Chairperson ASec. Christine Joy Cari.

The Commission resolution acknowledges the Philippines' commitment to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) and the Paris Agreement. It emphasizes the importance of Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE), a framework that promotes public education, training, awareness, participation, access to information, and international cooperation on climate change.

NYC recognizes the strong alignment between the ACE strategy and the recently published PYDP 2023-2028, particularly in the areas of environment and education.

Furthermore, the resolution calls for better governance in addressing climate change and highlights the need for a comprehensive national strategy. NYC urges the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) and the Climate Change Commission (CCC) to lead a Technical Working Group (TWG) or a similar mechanism tasked with developing and implementing the National ACE Strategy.

#AreaNews | NYC Baguio Inspires Youth Leadership at Nation Builders Leadership Summit 2024

The Nation Builders Leadership Summit 2024, organized by the Council for the Restoration of Filipino Values (CRFV) Inc., kicked off with a resounding focus on leadership and influence. Under the theme "Positioned to Influence," the summit gathered Sangguniang Kabataan Pederasyon Presidents, SK officials, Local Youth Development Officers, and public servants including Mayor Gina Merillo of Tanauan, Leyte, and Councilor Josie Creer.

Councilor Jose Molintas, representing City Mayor Benjamin Magalong, emphasized transformational leadership and the power of emotional intelligence in connecting with others. He highlighted the importance of empathy, support, and genuine care in effective leadership.

The keynote address, delivered by Ms. Sheridan Athena Gajete from the NYC Baguio Area Office, challenged traditional notions of leadership centered on titles and hierarchy. Ms. Gajete underscored that true leadership transcends formal positions by fostering connection, motivation, and inspiration among team members. She emphasized that inspiring others to lead themselves is the essence of impactful leadership.

The summit, designed to equip young leaders with essential skills, covers critical topics such as values-driven leadership, nation-building competencies, and decision-making and problem-solving. The event aims to empower youth to become effective leaders in their communities.

NTF ELCAC STATEMENT

There is no such thing as ‘redtagging’.  No less than the Supreme Court has ruled that there is no danger to life, liberty and security when one is identified as a member of the CPP NPA NDF.

The term redtagging is a tool used by the CPP NPA NDF fronts to silence those who are about to blow their cover. 

The time for silencing us is long past.  It is now time for the CPP NPA NDF to shut up. 

The NTF ELCAC stands firm in its duty of speaking the Truth to the Filipino people about the CPP NPA NDF and its numerous fronts whose main goal is to deceive them into joining these fronts and once in,  lead them down the road to  extremism with one goal and one goal alone: the violent overthrow of government and the installation of communism in the Philippines.

Countless lives have been sacrificed in the altar of the terrorist Joma Sison—our children, our indigenous peoples, the most vulnerable among us. 

The NTF ELCAC is successfully putting a stop to this tragic bloodbath. 

Already it has effected the surrender of almost 24,000 rebels who have peacefully laid down their arms—24,000 Filipinos no longer killing fellow Filipinos, 24,000 Filipinos rejoining their families and once again leading peaceful, productive lives. 

It has also effected the dismantling of an unprecedented number of whole guerrilla fronts and the institutionalizing of good governance— which is at the core of the NTF ELCAC as directed to us by our President who is the Chair of the Task Force.

We stand firm in ending this 53 year communist terrorist scourge and this also includes speaking the truth about politicians who form unholy alliances with the CPP NPA NDF and who lend them material support for their political ambitions at the expense of our country.

We will be unstinting with the Truth and we will  name them and not be party to their cover up. 

We will not be party to anything that prolongs the life of this communist terrorist group that has brought untold grief and destruction to our country—on no small measure brought on by the alliances made by traitors in our midst who pretend to be public servants while selling us and our children to these terrorists in exchange for whatever votes they can get from rotten  deals they make.

Peace is no longer a distant dream in the Philippines. 

In the countryside where numerous barangays have been cleared of the toxic presence of these terrorists, our countrymen are finally breathing the air of free men and women for the first time in decades.

Peace has become a reality and it has made a landfall in communities all over the country that have known nothing but the abuse and exploitation of the CPP NPA NDF. 

This obvious propaganda at the doorstep of the Ombudsman of the CPP NPA NDF fronts like KABATAAN, ANAKPAWIS, BAYAN MUNA, ALLIANCE OF CONCERNED TEACHERS (ACT), GABRIELA—collectively and aptly named KABAG— doesn’t even register with us. 

The Filipino people stand united with us in this fight for country and together we will put an end to the terrorist CPP NPA NDF. 

Undersecretary Lorraine Marie T. Badoy
NTF ELCAC Spokesperson for Sectoral Concerns

NTF-ELCAC: Telling the Truth is a National Security Imperative, Not Red-Tagging

Let it be clear that the state does not have a policy on red tagging.

Hence, the National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) takes exception that our efforts to expose individuals and organizations linked to the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People’s Army-National Democratic Front (CPP-NPA-NDF), amount to "red-tagging." The CPP-NPA-NDF are designated terrorist organizations under the Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020. It is not vilification when the government's claims are based on evidence, which are vetted by our security sector, of individuals and groups openly supporting or even recruiting for armed insurgency.  

Where there is fire, there is smoke. The associations of these individual groups go far beyond mere accusations. Over the years, not only did they have members who have embraced the path of violent armed insurgency, but their organizations openly romanticized their deaths. They mercilessly criticize the government for the mistakes its troops commit in the line of duty, yet are mum on the many cases where the CPP-NPA-NDF have committed grave crimes against the Filipino people. 

These organizations are quick to decry perceived injustices done to them but are eerily quiet about the many parents who have come forward to testify about how these supposedly legal organizations espoused illegal activities. More importantly, these very organizations, who claim to know everything about resolving the roots of social unrest, seem very antagonistic toward legitimate and proven counter-insurgency efforts of good governance, development, and social service programs that have resulted in the dismantling of the NPA's armed strength.

And now, they conveniently use the Supreme Court’s ruling on red-tagging cases while disregarding its clarification: labeling without basis is not allowed, but unmasking individuals with proven terrorist ties is a national security imperative. 

It is worth reiterating that the Philippine government does not engage in baseless accusations. Publicly-known former members of these organizations have courageously exposed the deceptive recruitment tactics of these organizations. Their testimonies have been and continue to be crucial in dismantling false narratives and in protecting our communities. The government remains supportive of social activism that encourages their members to become constructive citizens, not violent extremists.

On a final note, I would like to add that all governments label harmful substances like cigarettes and liquor because they endanger lives. Similarly, we identify individuals and organizations linked to communist terrorist groups because their recruitment and propaganda endanger the Filipino people, particularly the youth.

Undersecretary Ernesto C Torres Jr
Executive Director
NTF ECAC

This is the height of arrogance and stupidity on the part of those who are responsible for establishing the faulty community standards. Rest assured, your actions shall not be tolerated and shall not go unpunished.
If it will be proven after the conduct of an investigation that your actions (under the guise of fact-checking based not on facts but on something else as dictated by your principals) have resulted in hampering, preventing, hindering, obstructing, demeaning, vilifying, and undermining legitimate government efforts, programs, projects, and fact-based accomplishments by government agencies, proper actions will be undertaken against you and possible sanctions may be meted out on you.
This is not an attack on NTF-ELCAC alone. This is an attack against the Filipino people. This is an affront to the Philippine government. This is an attack against freedom of expression. READ | Statement On CPP-NPA-NDF Partylist Groups

The NTF-ELCAC would like to thank and congratulate the Filipino people for voting the CPP NPA NDF partylists- Bayan Muna and Anakpawis- OUT of Congress. 

The expulsion particularly of Bayan Muna is of notable worth because for 21 years, they have formidably sat in Congress, garnering the allowable maximum seats, 3–while using the immense powers of Congress to destroy our people and our country and wasting the precious time of our real Representatives in Congress with their filibustering and their constant obstruction of important legislative work meant to move our country forward and ease the burdens of the most marginalized among us. 

From over a million votes in the 2019 elections, Bayan Muna is on its way to extinction with just a little over 200k votes. 

We applaud the Filipino people for closing ranks behind our President, Chair of the NTF ELCAC, and heeding his call to defend and protect our country from these ruthless urban operatives of Joma Sison/CPP NPA NDF by fully using the power of your vote.

Truly, you understood so well that your vote is not only your RIGHT but your POWER. And you flexed it mightily. 

It is no exaggeration to say that you, our countrymen, have given the fiercest expression of your love for our country by the protection you have given the most helpless among us –our children, our indigenous brothers and sisters, the poorest among us–from some of their most merciless tormentors: legislators who pretend to care about human rights and have had the gall to call themselves ‘human rights defenders’ when they are, in fact, its worst offenders.

We would also like to thank the Filipino people for successfully slashing in half–from 6 to 3– the number of seats in the venerable House of Representatives for these urban operatives of the CPP NPA NDF. From around 3 million votes they garnered in the 2019 elections, they could only managed a little over a million votes in this year’s elections. 

We are saddened, however, that while all CPP NPA NDF Partylist groups registered a drastic reduction in the number of votes, Kabataan Partylist managed an increase to half a million votes. 

We attribute these votes to the treachery of Leni Robredo who colluded with the CPP NPA NDF who have co-opted Robredo followers in the Youth Sector and are now being radicalized and targeted for recruitment into this communist terrorist organization. 

For the record, if Leni Robredo had not conspired with this terrorist organization, there would have been one less seat in Congress for this terrorist organization and there would be far less kids radicalized and who are in grave danger of being led down the path of violent extremism that has killed an unbearable number of our children who have died as NPAs.

And so we continue this fight for our children and for country. 

Three more CPP NPA NDF Partylists are set to defile the Halls of Congress: Kabataan, Alliance of Concerned Teachers/ACT, Gabriela and so our work is not done here.

The NTF ELCAC has filed cases of disqualification and criminal cases against these CPP NPA NDF fronts and we are set on filing even more. 

And we would like to thank you in advance. 
You are fierce sons and daughters of the Republic and we are certain that it will not be long before we will finally and completely destroy this terrorist organization. 

Mabuhay ang matatapang na mga anak ng Republika na walang pag iimbot na nagmamahal ng buong tapang sa Kanya. 

Undersecretary Lorraine Marie T. Badoy
NTF ELCAC Spokesperson for Sectoral Concerns.
May 21, 2022

CORDILLERA RDRRMC STATEMENT ON THE NPA's ATTACK OF HADR OPERATIONS FOR ABRA EARTHQUAKE

The Cordillera Regional Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council strongly condemns in the strongest terms the NPA attack on our soldiers on their way to augment other troops in Licuan-Baay, Abra for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Response (HADR) Operations following the M 6.4 Earthquake in Abra.

The troops were ambushed by Communist Terrorist Group (CTG) killing two soldiers while one is still missing early morning today, October 27, 2022, in Brgy. Gacab, Malibcong, Abra.

This armed attack by the NPA against our soldiers in the exercises of their humanitarian assistance and disaster response (HADR) duties exposes their blatant disregard of the welfare of the Filipino people they claim to fight for.

We share our sympathy and condolences to the bereaved families of our fallen heroes.

NTF ELCAC STATEMENT ON BAYAN MUNA

CPP NPA NDF partylist Bayan Muna says their monumental loss in the 2022 elections- a first since it first perverted our electoral process 21 years ago- may have been caused by what it fancifully calls “massive vote shaving” instead of what it truly is- MASSIVE REJECTION BY THE FILIPINO PEOPLE who now know who it is they truly are: urban operatives of a terrorist organization who sit in Congress not to serve the Filipino people but to serve hand and foot the Central Committee of the CPP NPA NDF whose every bidding they obey in order to weaken government and destroy our democracy. 

Who shamelessly use government resources to destroy government and who use our democratic institutions to destroy our democracy. 

Their loss of about a million votes- from over a million in the 2019 elections to a pitiful 200k in the recently concluded polls- can be attributed to their loss of support from the people in the countryside where scores of our countrymen have turned their backs on them after finally feeling the services of government. 

Because indeed at the very core of the NTF ELCAC is GOOD GOVERNANCE. This is our President’s marching orders to us: “If you want this communist terrorist scourge to end, there are only two words here: good governance.”
And so this has been NTF ELCAC’s guiding light.

The Filipino people and the government that serves them have made good the social contract that exists between them and have become formidable in the face of this terrorist scourge that has brought Death and Destruction to the Motherland.

It is you, the Filipino people, that are delivering the death blow to our most ruthless, most inhumane oppressors: the CPP NPA NDF wearing the mask of Bayan Muna. 

The recently concluded elections will go down in Philippine history as the cleanest, most credible elections thus far and this will be etched in our country’s collective memory as one of the many precious gifts President Rodrigo Roa Duterte gave us. 

It will also be known as that chapter in our county’s history where an empowered people successfully booted out a massive half of the CPP NPA NDF in Congress signaling the start of the end of their 53 year reign of terror. 

Maraming salamat sa magigiting naming mga kababayan.

Mabuhay ang Pilipinong nagmamahal ng tunay sa Inang Bayan!

Mabuhay ang Ating Mahal na Pilipinas!

Undersecretary Lorraine Marie T. Badoy
NTF ELCAC Spokesperson for Social Media Affairs and Sectoral Concerns

READ | A Response to a Citizen's Opinion on the Appointment of Hon. Boying Remulla as the incoming Secretary of Justice

Prosecutor 2 Flosemer Chris Gonzales
Chairperson, Legal Cooperation Cluster 6
Spokesperson on Legal Affairs, NTF-ELCAC

Patricia Non in a statement said,
"Di ko alam kung anong justice system magkakaroon tayo." 
In the same statement, she criticized the pronouncements of Hon. Boying Remulla on the matter of the community pantries.

Non, however, took the statements of Hon. Remulla out of context.

Hon. Remulla categorically stated that community pantries should not be politicized, and should not be used as propaganda tool to criticize the government's CoVid response efforts.

Instead of taking the statements of Hon. Remulla as a legitimate reminder that their supposed initiatives should remain true to the bayanihan spirit, Non reacted negatively and made it appear that  Hon. Remulla's statements are unsupportive of their undertaking.

On Ms. Non's concern on what kind of justice system do we expect with Hon. Boying Remulla at the helm of the Department of Justice, here is what we can be certain of--

Incoming Justice Secretary Boying Remulla will uphold the rule of law,  and will demand no less than respect for the law. He will definitely require the full enforcement of the law under the principle that no one is above the law.

"Gusto nilang pag-away-awayin ang mga magsasaka para magulo ang bagong administrasyon," he explained.

NOT FROM TINANG

Meanwhile, Orlando Baluyot, Chairperson of the Tinang Samahang Nayon Multi-Purpose Cooperative, Inc., explained that the police where just doing their duty to protect crops owned by the cooperative that were deliberately being destroyed by the CTGs.

“Nung June 9, yung mga barangay officials sa amin ay nagsabi sa amin na may dalawang traktora na nandodoon na maninira ng tubo.  Kaagad po ay pumunta kami doon kasama ang mga board of directors naming,” Baluyot said, adding that this prompted them to call the police in order to stop the perpetrators from further destroying their crops.

“Simula't sapul pa lang na nandito kami sa Barangay Tinang ay hindi po kami naninira ng tao o nanloloko. Ang ginagawa lang po namin ay intindihin yung kabuhayan po ng mga miyembro po namin,” Baluyot added.  

Baluyot also said that he and the barangay officials did not recognize anybody in the group, saying that, “Yung karamihan po ng mga taong nandodoon ay hindi namin kilala, eto yung kinagulat naming… Hindi po namin alam kung sinu-sino ang mga iyan at kung anong mga grupo sila.”

On this note, Garcia criticized losing candidates Vice President Leni Robredo and Senator Kiko Pangilinan for allegedly supporting those who sowed chaos in Tinang.  According to him, “yung mga kabataan na involved sa Hacienda Tinang incident, ito ay yung mga supporters nila Leni at Kiko noong nakaraang eleksyon kaya nakikisawsaw sila… kahit mali na yung mga ginawa ng mga tao na ito, pilit pa nilang pinagtatanggol at pinagtatakpan at ipalabas na masama ang ating mga kapulisan.”

For her part, Spokesperson for Sectoral Concerns and Presidential Communications Undersecretary Lorraine Badoy said the results of the last elections plainly tell us that “walang tiwala ang taong bayan sa kanila,” referring to outgoing Vice President Robredo and Senator Pangilinan.

“Nakita nyo ba yung statements nila, nakakahiya, tuloy tuloy ang pakikipagsabwatan (nila).  Hindi ito about politics dahil wala na, talo na sila, pinakikita dito na kahit ang CPP ay nakikipagsabwatan sila,” Badoy said.

She added that based on the revelations made by Garcia and Sibayan, the people should be informed of the truth, that the Tinang incident was actually instigated by urban operatives of the CPP-NPA-NDF, and not just a dispute with the cooperative.  

Assistant Provincial Prosecutor Flosemer Chris Gonzales, Acting NTF-ELCAC Spokesperson on Legal Affairs, on the other hand, warned the youth not to be used by the CTGs for their propaganda. He pointed out that in truth, the CTGs were the ones who started the incident, then they will use that same incident to ask for funding from abroad. This has been the same modus operandi that they have been using since the 70s to the detriment of the country.

PNP WELCOMES CHR PROBE ON INCIDENT

Meanwhile, the Philippine National Police (PNP) in Region 3 has welcomed the filing of complaints with the Commission on Human Rights (CHR) against any of their personnel involved in the arrest of individuals behind the “Bungkalan” mobilization in Hacienda Tinang in Concepcion, Tarlac.

PNP-PRO3 Director, Brig. Gen. Matthew Baccay, said the June 10 incident in Tinang  initially netted 91 individuals including five foreigners and four minors who were eventually released, leaving 82 offenders on charges of 'malicious mischiefs' and illegal assembly.  All those charged were also released on bail.

Baccay said the detention of the 82 individuals was “in accordance with the law and with the utmost respect for human rights.”

"We have done only our duty… I will stand by my men to answer the charges filed against us," Baccay said.

The official was referring to charges filed before the CHR over allegations of abuse in the arrest and detention, and the delay in the release of the offenders.

BUONG HANAY NG NPA SA SURIGAO DEL NORTE, SUMUKO

Gigaquit, Surigao del Norte – Buong hanay ng Sandatahang Yunit Pangpropaganda (SYP) 16B ng Guerilla Front16 (GF16) ng NorthEastern Mindanao Regional Committee ng NPA sumuko dala ang kanilang iba’t-ibang matataas na kalibre ng baril sa himpilan ng 30IB sa bayan ng Gigaquit, Surigao del Norte nitong ika-22 ng Agosto 2022. 

Ang grupo ay kinabibilangan nila Panie Hukman Jubasan alyas ALVIN O JIMBOY, Junas Lubang Morata alyas BONG, Merimi Cortes Manego alyas ODETTE at Lenie B Mantawiran alyas GLAI-GLAI pawang mga miyembro ng SYP16B, GF16. Gutom at pangungulila sa kanilang pamilya at ang pinaigting at walang hintong operasyon ng 30IB ang pangunahing dahilan ng kanilang pagsuko.

Ang kanilang pagsuko ay mapayapang tinanggap ni Lt.Col Jerold H Jale ang pinuno ng 30IB na agad nagbigay ng kaukulang panahon upang makapiling and kani-kanilang mga pamilya habang inaayos ang mga dokumento upang makatanggap ng agarang tulong pinansyal sa ilalim ng Enhance Comprehensive Local Integration Program O E-CLIP ng gobyerno.

Kasabay ng kanilang pagbabalik-loob ay ang pagsuko ng kanilang mga armas at pagbunyag ng lokasyon kung saan nila tinago sa bandang Huwat Baboy, Brgy Jubgan, San Francisco at bandang Brgy Cagtinae, Malimono ang mga natitira pa nilang mga armas. Naibalik sa pwersa ng gobyerno ang labing limang (15) matataas na kalibre ng baril katulad ng limang (5) M16 Rifle, anim (6) na M4 Rifle, isang (1) M203 Grenade Launcher, isang (1) R4 Rifle, isang (1) AK47 Rifle at isang (1) Galil Rifle. Bukod sa matataas na armas, isinuko din ang iba nila and iba pang kagamitang pangdigma tulad ng mga magazine na may bilang na limamput pito (57), ibat-ibang uri ng mga bala, iligal na pampasabog (IED), isang (1) binocular, watawat ng NPA, personal na mga kagamitan at mga subersibong dokumento ng NPA.

Ang nangyaring pagbabalik-loob nina alyas Jimboy, alyas Bong, alyas Odette at alyas Glai-Glai ang naging dahilan upang tuluyang mabuwag ang grupo ng SYP 16B ng GF16 dahil kung matatandaan natin nitong ika-12 ng Agosto, siyam (9) sa kanilang kasamahan ang naunang bumaba na may dalang mga matataas na kalibre ng armas na may bilang na labing anim (16).

Ayon kay Panie Hukman Jubasan alyas ALVIN O JIMBOY, nagpapasalamat siya sa kasundaluhan ng 30IB na inakalang saktan sila ngunit sa halip ay nirespeto at inalagaan sila na parang isang kapatid.

“Nagapasalamat ko sa 30IB ilabi na sa ilang Batcom kay gidawat mi nila isip usa ka igsuon ug wala mi pasakiti. Ilang girespeto ang among tawhanong katungod ug desiyon sa pagbalik sa sabakan sa gobyerno. “Nagpapasalamat ako sa 30IB lalo na sa kanilang Batcom sa pagtanggap sa amin bilang isang kapatid at hindi kami sinaktan. Nirespeto nila ang aming karapatang pantao pati ang aming desisyon na manumbalik sa gobyerno”.

Ipinabatid din ni Lt.Col Jerold H Jale na ang kanilang pagsuko ay isang tagumpay hindi lang para sa kasundaluhan kundi pati na rin sa mga pamahalaang lokal at mamamayan sa probinsya ng Surigao del Norte”.

“Ang pagsuko ng buong pwersa ng SYP16B ay isang makasaysayang tagumpay ng bawat ahensiya ng gobyerno dito sa probinsya ng Surigao del Norte. Bunga din ito ng patuloy na pakikipag-tulungan at kooperasyon mula sa mga mamamayan. Asahan din natin na ang minimithi nating kapayapaan at pag-unlad ay tuluyan nating makamit". pahayag ni Lt. Col. Jale.

Sa ngayon, ang mga sumukong NPA ay patuloy pa din sa sinasagawang oryentasyon para sa kanilang pag enroll sa Enhanced Comprehensive Local Integration Program o E-CLIP.

From the 30th Infantry "Fight On" Battalion, Philippine Army

READ | PRESS STATEMENT
(Re: CHR's Report on Investigated Killings in Relation to the Anti-Illegal Drug Campaign)

In its Report on Investigated Killings in Relation to the Anti-Illegal Drug Campaign on April 2022, the Commission on Human Rights (CHR) concluded, in fine, that “majority” of the “killings” were done “outside the context of law enforcement operation” and that the government “failed in its obligation to respect and protect the human rights of every citizen” as well as “encouraged a culture of impunity”. 

Such conclusion is bereft of any factual or legal bases, it is nothing but an attempt to discredit and malign the government, both locally and abroad, and seeks to push the narrative that the killings were “sanctioned” by the State. However, nowhere in the CHR’s 48-page Report is such conclusion supported by clear evidence – all but mere assumptions and surmises of CHR. If any, the deaths can be attributed to legal police operations, or at most, that the police officers on the ground may be liable. However, it needs a case to be filed in court to prove, and not for the CHR to conclude that it is “sanctioned” by the State.

The CHR Report mentions the statements of alleged witnesses to the purported killings, but there is no thorough investigation conducted on the backgrounds, motives, and/or the credibility of these purported witnesses. Otherwise, if these witnesses are reliable, then the CHR is duty bound to recommend charges against the alleged erring police officers instead of erroneously and irresponsibly putting the blame to the State.

It is noteworthy that the CHR did not include in their Report the fact that many of the deaths in the drug war were perpetrated by drug syndicates and drug groups themselves who kill their competitors. There is also no mention of the law enforcement officers and agents who lost their lives in the anti-drug campaign during this period. It is as if only the drug peddlers have human rights, but not the police officers who gave their lives in the line of duty to protect us.

Instead of trying to paint a dark picture of the so-called drug war, the CHR should have included, for the purpose of providing proper context, the data on the number of arrests, cases filed in court, the volume of drugs confiscated,  the number of drug surrenderers under Oplan Tokhang, the data on those who availed of rehabilitation, those who underwent Community Based Rehabilitation Programs, the Balay Silangan project, and the decrease in the crime rate during the same period. 

The CHR, being an integral part of the government, should not demonize the very government it ought to serve and protect, more so, without basis. Rather than maligning it, the CHR should assist the government to protect the human rights of the police officers and the innocent public against the menace of illegal drugs, and to apply a fair approach before condemning the government without due process of law.

READ | DILG defends NSC and NTC decision to block Communist terrorist websites 

The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) today defended the decision of the National Security Council (NSC) and the National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) to restrict the websites of CPP-NPA-NDF affiliated groups and front organizations. 

DILG Undersecretary and NTF ELCAC Spokesperson Jonathan Malaya said that the legal basis in restricting these websites primarily emanates from RA No. 11479 or more popularly known as "The Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020" particularly in the first paragraph of Sec. 2, which reads:
 
Section 2. Declaration of Policy.- It is declared a policy of the State to protect life, liberty, and property from terrorism, to condemn terrorism as inimical and dangerous to the national security of the country and to the welfare of the people, and to make terrorism a crime against the Filipino people, against humanity, and against The Law of Nations.
 
Malaya said that it is clear that “terrorism” is declared explicitly as a crime, not only against the Filipino people but also of humanity and the laws of nations. In this connection, the Anti-Terrorism Council (ATC) has issued three (3) resolutions declaring the CPP-NPA-NDF and their central committee members as terrorists and terrorist organizations pursuant to par. 3 of Sec. 25 of the said law.

“In light of the declaration of policy of RA 11479, the resolution of the ATC, and in order to implement the same,  restricting or blocking the web-sites of the declared terrorist organizations and their members are in accordance with law,” he said. 
 
“It cannot be denied that the propaganda contained in the terrorist web-sites are being used to advance the illegal cause of the terrorists, in this case the CPP-NPA-NDF, which is to overthrow the government through violent means and destroy our democratic way of life. I wish to emphasize that inciting to commit terrorism is a criminal act in Sec.9 of RA 11479, which reads:  Section 9. Inciting to Commit Terrorism.- Any person who, without taking any direct part in the commission of terrorism, shall include others to the execution of any of the acts specified in Section 4 hereof by means of speeches, proclamations, writings, emblems, banners or other representations tending to the same end,” he added. 
 
He said that those affected by this sanction may advance the argument that the same is a curtailment of their freedom of speech. However,  this should fail because freedom of speech is not absolute as held by the Supreme Court in Gonzales v. COMELEC, 137 Phil. 471, 494. 

“Some types of speech may be subjected to some regulation by the State under its police power, in order that it may not be injurious to the equal right of others or those of the community or society,” he said. 
 
Malaya said that even the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights recognizes that freedom of speech and expression has limitations, particularly on issues of national security and public order, which is the very purpose why the terrorist CPP-NPA-NDF websites are being blocked.

MONUMENT FOR KIETH ABSALON UNVEILED IN MASBATE

Serving as a perpetual reminder of football star Kieth Absalon who was mercilessly killed by the New People's Army (NPA), the armed wing of the Communist Party of the Philippines - National Democratic Front (CPP-NDF) exactly a year from today (June 6), a monument to honor his exemplary life was unveiled at the Masbate Sports Complex.

In commemoration of his first death anniversary, Vilma Absalon, the mother of Kieth, said, "Ito ay magpapa-alala sa aking anak. Napakasakit man ng pangyayari na di malilimutan kailanman. Magsisilbi ito sa mga kabataan, na ang sports ay maaaring daan para makapag-aral,” during a special press briefing of the National Task Force to End Local Communists Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) also held in Masbate.

"His short-lived life will serve as an inspiration to Masbateños, that there is a future in sports. I am really happy to be part of this commemoration, and the journey of Kieth and his education, how he got to FEU," Nene Merioles, Masbate School Superintendent who also knew Absalon as a student, said.

Merioles said that even before the pandemic and the tragic incident that befell the Absalon cousins, provincial schools have been encouraging students to be involved in sports, citing Kieth's achievements, who even at a young age of 12-years-old was already a scholar. Through football, Kieth was also able to get a college scholarship with the Far Eastern University (FEU) where he was eventually awarded the Most Valuable Player (MVP) title.

Investigation revealed that Kieth, together with his cousin Nolven and some friends and relatives were biking in the area when the NPA deliberately detonated an anti-personnel mine. Kieth and Nolven were severely wounded and as they were lying on the ground with their hands raised, the two begged for mercy.  But their pleas fell on deaf ears when they were mercilessly shot to death by NPA rebels whose aim was to sow fear and terror in Masbate.  At the time of his death, Kieth was only 21 years old. 

The NPA thereafter issued a statement admitting the killing of the Absalons.

The Monument

Before the unveiling of the monument, NTF-ELCAC spokesperson and Communications Undersecretary Lorraine Marie Badoy read the inscription during the presscon.

In part, the inscription read, “Kieth took his place and stood shoulder to shoulder with other gladiators and kicked soil to make that ball rocket to the stratosphere and to countless victories. He showed us just how beautiful it is when we are able to keep promises we make to ourselves and make our dreams happen. In that moment Kieth was transcendent. And there was much he taught us.” 

“His light was momentarily dimmed by monsters who have stolen our children from us and eaten them whole. And there was nothing we could do to protect them. We weep at this loss and the loss of each child we allowed to be devoured by this evil beast. Police, Army update

The Philippine National Police (PNP) meanwhile, gave an update in the case and said that they have already arrested two suspects, most recently Rolly Hermina, an NPA fighter belonging to Larangan 1, Komite ng Probinsya 4, under the command of Eddie Rosero, alias “Ka Star,” operating in the second district of the province.

Colonel Rayan Ador, Chief of the Regional Investigation and Detective Management Division of Police Regional Office 5, said intensified manhunt operations are continuing to get the other suspects.

LtCol. Siegfried Felipe Awichen, Commander of the 2nd Infantry Brigade said a year passed and they are still awaiting the Communist Terrorist Group (CTG) to make good their promise to hand over the perpetrators of these heinous killings.

"We're still conducting continuous operations to hunt them down and bring to justice those who are responsible. Series of encounters have severely disrupted the CTG operations in the province," Felipe said.

LtC Benjamin Lonogan, Commander of the 96th Infantry Brigade, 9ID on the other hand, encouraged Masbateños to assist them in eradicating the communist scourge in Masbate which has brought nothing but misery and hardship to the people.

Mother's plea

Absalon's mom, Vilma after acknowledging those who helped built the monument, particularly officials of NTF-ELCAC and other government agencies, pleaded that the next administration of newly elected President Ferdinand "Bong Bong" Marcos Jr. to continue outgoing President Rodrigo Roa Duterte’s whole-of-nation approach to end the more than five-decades of terrorism courtesy of the CTGs.

"Ipagpatuloy po sana ninyo ang NTF-ELCAC at ang aming laban sa mga komunistang-terorista, para sa ating mga kabataan," Absalon's mother pleaded.

Local government units (LGUs) played a crucial role in stopping local poll candidates from giving in to the extortion attempts of communist terrorist groups (CTGs) in the last elections.

This was revealed by the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) Undersecretary Jonathan Malaya in Monday's NTF-ELCAC virtual press briefing focusing on the "successful eradication of terror threats of CTGs towards politicians."

Malaya explained, “This is a combination of factors: the support of the local government units, our focused military and police operations, the retooled community support programs, the support to the barangay development programs. So, this was a combination of supply and demand, the supply has diminished, the demand has diminished and therefore we have this successful conduct of the elections.”

Malaya, who is also the NTF ELCAC Spokesperson on Local Government Affairs and Barangay Development Program validated the report made by DILG Secretary Eduardo Año to President Rodrigo Duterte that there are no reports of politicians or LGU officials paying permits to campaign fees or any form of aid to the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People’s Army-National Democratic Front (CPP-NPA-NDF) in this year's elections.

“Magandang report ito sa Pangulo ni Secretary Año," Malaya said, adding that the early warning issued by Año that politicians who would give in to the demands of CPP-NPA-NDF would be penalized for violating Republic Act 10168, otherwise known as the "Terrorism Financing Prevention and Suppression Act of 2012", Section 261 of the Omnibus Election Code and Article 137 of the Revised Penal Code or the Disloyalty of Public Officers.
